When the user uses a weekday shortcut (for example, selecting the next Wednesday), compute the next calendar date matching that weekday, ensure it falls within or extends the current date range, and if necessary call getVisitAvailability with an extended range that includes the computed date before updating selectedDate.
When the user selects a time slot, mark that slot as selected in state (referenced by its id) and update the visitor details form section to indicate the chosen time.

When the user edits visitor details (parentName, parentPhone, reminderMethod), update a form model in state and run client-side validation checks (e.g., required fields, phone format) before enabling submission.

When the user submits the booking form, validate that a date, time slot, and required visitor details are present; then call createVisitBooking with nurseryId, visitSlotId, visitDate, startTime, endTime, parentName, parentPhone, and reminderMethod from state.

On successful createVisitBooking response, update state with the returned booking, change the page mode to summary, and optionally update the URL to include visitBookingId=booking.id (using history replacement or redirect) so that reloading the page can reconstruct the summary via getVisitBookingSummary.

On unsuccessful createVisitBooking response (e.g., slot conflict indicated by the backend), show an error message, optionally refresh availability for the affected date with getVisitAvailability, and keep the form data so the user can select another slot.
In summary mode, when the user clicks the "Finish"/"Done" button, navigate to visits.html using a link or redirect as defined in the outgoing navigation.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page state is driven entirely by URL parameters and in-memory state derived from the three interfaces. On initial load, the page reads the URL parameters nurseryId, visitDate, and visitBookingId. If visitBookingId is present, the page enters 'summary' mode regardless of other parameters: it calls getVisitBookingSummary(visitBookingId) and uses the returned booking and nursery to populate the summary and context components; nurseryId and visitDate from the URL are ignored for data loading to avoid conflicts. If visitBookingId is absent and nurseryId is present, the page enters 'booking' mode: it calls getVisitAvailability with nurseryId and a configurable future date range, stores the returned nursery and availableSlots, and uses optional visitDate (if present) to set the initial selectedDate. If neither visitBookingId nor nurseryId is present, the page cannot function; it should display an error state explaining that the nursery context is missing and provide navigation back to visits.html. Internal, non-persistent state includes: currentMode (booking or summary), selectedDate, selectedVisitSlotId, visitorDetails (parentName, parentPhone, reminderMethod), and booking (VisitBooking) once created or loaded. When the user selects a date beyond the currently loaded availability range, the page recomputes a new date range around that date and issues a new getVisitAvailability call; the new response replaces or extends availableSlots in state. After a successful createVisitBooking call, the page updates its booking state with the returned VisitBooking and switches currentMode to summary. Optionally, it updates the URL to add or replace visitBookingId with booking.id so that refreshing the page or bookmarking the URL will restore the summary via getVisitBookingSummary. No booking or nursery data is stored in localStorage; the URL parameters and server interfaces are the sole sources of truth across page loads.

components.functionality: Coordinates page initialization, mode selection (booking vs summary), API calls, and high-level state. On load, reads nurseryId, visitDate, and visitBookingId from the URL. If visitBookingId is present, calls getVisitBookingSummary and routes data to the nursery context and summary components. If visitBookingId is absent but nurseryId is present, calls getVisitAvailability with a computed startDate and endDate, sets the initial selected date (optionally from visitDate), and routes data to the availability and form components. Manages transitions from booking mode to summary mode after createVisitBooking completes and can update the URL with visitBookingId to reflect the new booking.

Displays basic information about the nursery for which a visit is being booked or reviewed. Shows the nursery name and any other essential context fields that are available in the Nursery object. In booking mode, it receives the Nursery from getVisitAvailability; in summary mode, it receives the Nursery from getVisitBookingSummary. It does not call interfaces itself; it passively renders data provided by the state manager.

Encapsulates the logic to load and refresh visit availability for a nursery over a date range. When instructed by the state manager (for example, on initial load or when the user navigates to a date outside the current range), it calls getVisitAvailability with nurseryId, startDate, and endDate. It passes the resulting nursery and availableSlots back to the state manager. It handles loading and error states for these calls.

Allows the user to select the calendar date for the visit. Receives the current selectedDate and a derived mapping of dates that have availableSlots from the state manager. Renders an interactive date control and ensures that when a date is selected, it emits a date-change event. Also provides weekday shortcuts (e.g., a selector to choose a weekday and a 'next' action) that compute the next calendar date matching that weekday; if this calculated date is outside the currently loaded range, the event includes a flag that the state manager uses to trigger a new getVisitAvailability call before updating selectedDate.

Shows the list of available time slots for the currently selected date. Receives selectedDate and the full availableSlots array from the state manager and filters slots by date, optionally also excluding any slots where isBooked is true if such flags are present. Displays each candidate slot with startTime and endTime. When the user clicks a slot, it emits a slot-selected event containing the slot's id and time information so the state manager can update selectedVisitSlotId and the visitDate/time used for createVisitBooking.

Collects the visitor's personal details required for the booking: parentName, parentPhone, and reminderMethod. Receives the current selected slot and date from the state manager so it can display a summary of the chosen visit time within the form for context. Performs client-side validation of required fields and format before allowing submission. When submitted, it emits a booking-submit event with the current form values, which the state manager then combines with selectedVisitSlotId and date/time to call createVisitBooking.

Responsible for executing the booking creation call. Listens for booking-submit events from the visitor-details-form (via the state manager). When triggered, it calls createVisitBooking with the nurseryId from URL/state, visitSlotId from the selected slot, visitDate and startTime/endTime from the selected slot and date, plus parentName, parentPhone, and reminderMethod from the form model. On success, it returns the booking and message to the state manager so that booking can be stored in state and the page can switch to summary mode. On failure, it relays error information so the UI can present a meaningful error and allow the user to try a different slot or correct their details.

Displays a read-only summary of the visit booking. In summary mode, the state manager either provides the VisitBooking and Nursery from getVisitBookingSummary (if visitBookingId is in the URL) or from the result of createVisitBooking (for immediate post-booking display). It shows the nursery name, visit date and time range, parentName, parentPhone, reminderMethod, and booking status using the fields of VisitBooking and Nursery. It does not allow editing or re-submission; it is purely for confirmation and review.

Loads an existing booking summary when visitBookingId is present in the URL. On initialization (or when the visitBookingId portion of the URL changes), it calls getVisitBookingSummary with that id. It passes the resulting booking and nursery to the state manager, which then populates the booking-summary-view and nursery-context-panel. Handles error and loading states specific to summary retrieval.

Provides navigation actions for completing or leaving the booking flow. In summary mode, it renders a "Finish" or "Done" button that, when clicked, navigates the user to visits.html according to the outgoing navigation specification. It may also optionally render a 'Back' or 'Change booking' action that returns to booking mode only when appropriate (for example, for a booking that has just been created and while in the same session), using the state manager to adjust mode without relying on localStorage.

Shows loading indicators and error messages for API interactions managed by this page. Listens for loading and error events from the state manager related to getVisitAvailability, createVisitBooking, and getVisitBookingSummary. When an error occurs, it displays a generic or API-provided message and offers a retry control that triggers the appropriate data-loader component to re-call the failed interface.

layout.grid_system: Appears to follow a centered, fixed-width container on a fluid background, likely based on a 12‑column grid. The welcome section uses a two‑column grid (approx. 7 columns text, 5 columns illustration).
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows the mockup’s linear top-to-bottom flow with a prominent hero-like booking header at the top inside a rounded rectangle, followed by a main two-column booking section. The hero spans the content width within the centered container and introduces the Visit Booking flow with a large title and short copy. Immediately below, the main section uses a left–right split: the left column (about 60% of content width) contains the interactive booking steps (date, time, visitor details, flow actions), and the right column (about 40%) contains contextual information (nursery details) and the live booking summary. A floating, always-accessible primary action area at the lower-right of the left column echoes the mockup’s anchored bottom-right element.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows the mockup’s linear top-to-bottom flow with a prominent hero-like booking header at the top inside a rounded rectangle, followed by a main two-column booking section. The hero spans the content width within the centered container and introduces the Visit Booking flow with a large title and short copy. Immediately below, the main section uses a left–right split: the left column (about 60% of content width) contains the interactive booking steps (date, time, visitor details, flow actions), and the right column (about 40%) contains contextual information (nursery details) and the live booking summary. A floating, always-accessible primary action area at the lower-right of the left column echoes the mockup’s anchored bottom-right element.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The page is built on a centered, fixed-width container (e.g., max-width ~1120–1200px) on a fluid, softly colored background. The hero occupies the full container width with generous vertical padding (32–48px). Below it, the main booking section reserves ~55–60% width for the left interactive column and ~40–45% for the right contextual column, separated by a 24–32px gutter. Each major card (date selector, time slots, visitor form, nursery panel, summary) uses 24–32px internal padding and 24–32px vertical gaps between them. Vertical spacing between the hero and main section follows the 48–64px section margin pattern.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density is intentionally moderate to low: large, readable labels; generous line heights; and comfortable gaps between fields and list items. Each card has plenty of whitespace around its content, mirroring the editorial, child-friendly feel. The date picker, time slots, and form fields are arranged in a single column with clear separation (16–24px) between controls. The right column’s context and summary cards are spacious, with ample breathing room around headings and icons/illustrations.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow mirrors the mockup’s Z-pattern: the eye starts at the large hero heading in the top-left of the rounded header, then moves to the right side of the hero where any small helper text or decorative illustration sits. From there, it drops down to the left column where the primary interaction steps align vertically (date, then time, then details). As the user scans down the left, the right column’s colorful context and summary card act as a stable secondary focal area. The main confirmation action is aligned toward the bottom-right of the left column, echoing the mockup’s subtle anchor in the bottom-right corner.

layout.overall_description: The Visit Booking page sits within a softly colored, child-friendly background, with the main content centered in a rounded, fixed-width container. At the top, a hero-style header card with rounded corners and generous padding introduces the booking process in large, warm typography, perhaps with a small, playful illustration on the right side to mirror the hero slider feel. Beneath this, the page resolves into a balanced two-column layout: on the left, a vertical stack of spacious cards guides the user through choosing a visit date, selecting a time slot, and filling in visitor details, each step clearly delineated with headings and comfortable spacing. On the right, a taller, single column contains a nursery context panel and a booking summary card, styled like friendly, illustrated info cards that remain visible as the user scrolls. A slim feedback banner appears just under the hero when there are loading states or errors, and a compact, rounded action area with the main confirmation button anchors the lower-right of the left column, always close at hand. The overall effect is an open, playful, and reassuring booking journey that closely echoes the mockup’s hero-plus-two-column welcome section.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This page-controller component does not render a visible UI element. Conceptually, it wraps the entire Visit Booking page, occupying the full width of the centered content container from the hero area down through the main two-column section. It orchestrates the state for availability loading, summary loading, and booking creation, but has no direct spatial footprint; the space it governs is visually expressed through the date selector, time slot list, visitor form, summary view, and feedback banner that it coordinates.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual and non-interactive; it behaves as an invisible wrapper around the page content, influencing what appears within other components but not taking any space itself.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The nursery context panel sits at the top of the right column of the main booking section, directly below the hero and any feedback banner. It occupies roughly 40–45% of the container width, aligned flush to the right with a 24–32px gutter separating it from the left interactive column. The panel itself is a rounded-corner card with 24–32px internal padding, containing the nursery name, address, and possibly a small, playful illustration or icon that echoes the warm, watercolor style. Vertically, it takes up the top 25–30% of the right column content, leaving room below for the booking summary. It aligns to the top of the viewport when the user scrolls so it feels like a stable reference for where the visit will occur.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Positioned within the right column and may use a sticky behavior: it sticks near the top of the viewport (e.g., 24px offset from the hero’s lower edge) so that the nursery context stays visible while the left column content scrolls.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The visit availability loader manifests inline within the left column in place of the date selector and time slot list when data is being fetched. It uses skeleton blocks that match the approximate width of the left column (about 55–60% of the container) and the expected height of the date picker and a few time slot rows. These skeletons are housed inside the same rounded card footprints as the actual components, with 24–32px padding, so the layout remains stable: a card at the top of the left column where the date selector will appear, followed by another card where the time slots will load, separated by a 24px vertical gap.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline and non-intrusive; it occupies the same spatial areas as the date selector and time slot list, preventing layout shift. It does not overlay other sections or block interaction with the right column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The visit date selector is the first interactive card in the left column of the main section, directly below the hero and any feedback banner. It spans the full width of the left column (approximately 55–60% of the container width) with a soft, rounded rectangle shape and internal padding of 24–32px on all sides. A large, friendly heading such as “Válaszd ki a látogatás napját” sits at the top left of the card, occupying the top 20–25% of its height, with one or two short helper lines beneath. The calendar grid or date scroller fills the remaining vertical space, with generous row heights and 8–16px gaps between weeks or date rows to maintain the open, child-friendly feel. A 24–32px vertical gap separates this card from the time slot list below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the left column; it scrolls normally with the page, always aligned to the left column’s grid and maintaining its width and padding at different viewport sizes within the main container.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The time slot list sits directly below the date selector card in the left column, separated by a 24–32px vertical gap. It also spans the full width of the left column and appears as a rounded card with 24–32px internal padding. A clear subheading such as “Válaszd ki az időpontot” sits near the card’s top left, occupying about the top 15–20% of its height. Below this, time slots are arranged in a single vertical column of pill-like buttons or list rows, each with generous vertical padding (8–12px) and 16–24px spacing between rows. If there are many slots, the list may scroll internally within the card up to roughly half the viewport height, maintaining a sense of airiness rather than becoming cramped. When a slot is selected, the state is visually emphasized but still fits comfortably within the open layout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the left column and directly dependent on the date selection above; it scrolls with the rest of the card stack, maintaining its relative position beneath the date selector.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The visitor details form occupies the lower section of the left column, positioned beneath the time slot list with a 32–48px vertical gap to clearly signal the next step. It is contained in a large, rounded card spanning the full width of the left column, with substantial internal padding (32px ideal) to accommodate multiple fields without feeling crowded. A prominent heading such as “Látogató adatai” appears at the top, with a brief explanatory line beneath; together they take up roughly the top quarter of the card. Below, form fields (name, contact details, notes, etc.) are arranged in a single column with 16–24px vertical spacing and full-width inputs, preserving the spacious, friendly feel. If there are grouped fields (e.g., phone and email), they may sit side by side only on wide screens, but still with generous padding. The bottom of this card aligns visually near the area where the booking flow actions will appear, keeping the end of the form and the confirmation controls in the same visual band.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the left column; as the longest card, it drives the scroll length of the left side while the right column context can remain visible via stickiness.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The create booking handler does not appear as a visible element; it is functionally tied to the main confirmation action in the booking flow actions area. Conceptually, it sits just behind the primary “Foglalás létrehozása” button at the bottom-right of the left column, with its logic governing the transition from filled form and selected slot to the confirmed booking state shown in the summary. It therefore shares the spatial context of the booking flow actions without adding any extra visual surface.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual and event-driven; it occupies no independent space and activates in place when the user triggers the main confirmation action.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The booking summary view sits in the right column directly below the nursery context panel, separated by a 24–32px vertical gap. It shares the same right-column width (about 40–45% of the container) and appears as a rounded, lightly elevated card with 24–32px internal padding. The top of the card features a bold, friendly heading such as “Foglalás összefoglaló” on the left, possibly accompanied by a small, playful icon or illustration on the right, echoing the mockup’s use of imagery as a secondary focal point. Below, key booking details (selected date, time, visitor name, number of visitors) are presented as a vertical list with 12–16px gaps between items, each line using clear labels and slightly larger values to maintain readability. The card height adjusts with content but typically fills 40–60% of the right column’s vertical space, visually balancing the taller left column. The summary updates dynamically as the user interacts with the left column, serving as a stable reference panel that the eye returns to often.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Located within the right column, often within a sticky container shared with the nursery context panel so that the entire right column’s content remains visible or partially visible while the user scrolls the left column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: When the booking summary is loading or refreshing, the booking summary loader appears as a skeleton or subtle shimmer inside the same card footprint as the booking summary view in the right column. It preserves the same width and approximate height, with horizontal bars representing the heading and each summary line. This ensures that, visually, the right column remains occupied by a summary-shaped card even while data is being fetched, avoiding sudden changes in layout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline within the booking summary card area in the right column; it replaces the summary content temporarily but does not alter the size or position of the card.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The booking flow actions sit at the bottom of the left column, closely associated with the end of the visitor details form. On initial load, they appear directly beneath the form card, with a 24px vertical gap creating a clear separation. The actions occupy a horizontal strip that spans the left column width, but the key buttons themselves are grouped toward the right side to mirror the mockup’s bottom-right anchor: a secondary action (e.g., “Vissza” or “Mégse”) aligned to the center-right and the primary confirmation button aligned to the far right, both within a shared baseline. Internal button padding follows the 8–12px guideline, and there is 16–24px spacing between buttons. On taller viewports, this action strip can use sticky positioning relative to the bottom of the viewport, so that as the user scrolls within the left column, the primary button remains visible near the lower-right corner of the content area, echoing the floating scroll-to-top control from the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Anchored to the left column and may behave as a bottom-sticky bar: it stays pinned near the bottom of the visible left column area (e.g., 24px above the viewport bottom) while the user scrolls, ensuring constant access to the main action without obscuring other content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The loading and error banner appears as a slim, full-width bar inside the centered main container, positioned directly below the hero header and above the two-column booking section. It spans nearly 100% of the container width with rounded corners and 12–16px internal vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding. In loading or informational states it may use a warm, pale yellow background with friendly iconography, while error states may use a soft red tone, both fitting the playful, non-threatening style. The banner pushes the main booking content down but maintains the same top and side margins, preserving the clean vertical rhythm established by the hero and sections. When not needed, this space collapses entirely, returning the two-column section to sit just under the hero.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static and inline between the hero and main content; it appears and disappears by expanding/collapsing in place, without overlaying other content or changing the width of the columns.

name: Activities & Holiday Programs
title: Activities & Holiday Programs
description: Search, filter, and select municipal daycare activities and holiday programs, view activity details, and add selected activities to a registration list.

core_features: Load dynamic filter options for age groups, available months, times of day, and suggested price ranges using getActivityFilterOptions.
Allow users to filter activities by age range, month, time of day, and maximum price per session and reflect the chosen filters in the URL parameters.
Search activities and holiday programs using searchActivities based on the current filter state and display them as a list of activity cards with center information.
Group or visually cluster activities by center (using centerId/centerName from searchActivities results) so users can see multiple activities at the same center.
Let users open a dedicated activity detail page from any activity card, passing along a returnQuery string that preserves current filters (and sorting) in the URL.
Enable users to add activities directly to a registration list from activity cards via addActivityToRegistrationList, providing or reusing childName and numChildren.
Show a registration list summary/indicator (e.g., total items) that updates whenever addActivityToRegistrationList is called, and provide a link to activity_registration.html.
Maintain list state (filters and sorting) exclusively via URL parameters so that links can be shared and users can return from detail/registration pages to the same filtered list.
user_workflows:
Workflow 1: Landing on the page with or without predefined filters
1. User opens activities.html, possibly with URL parameters (ageMinYears, ageMaxYears, month, timeOfDay, maxPricePerSession).
2. url-state-manager component parses URL parameters and exposes an initial filter state.
3. activity-filters-form initializes by calling getActivityFilterOptions and maps the returned options (ageGroups, monthOptions, timeOfDayOptions, priceRangeSuggestions) to its controls.
4. activity-filters-form preselects filter controls using the parsed URL parameters where applicable.
5. activity-results-list uses the current filter state to call searchActivities (passing ageMinYears, ageMaxYears, month, timeOfDay, maxPricePerSession; startDate and endDate are omitted/undefined on this page).
6. activity-results-list renders activity-card instances for each returned activity and groups/labels them by centerName/centerId.
7. registration-list-indicator remains empty or hidden if no addActivityToRegistrationList call has occurred during this session.

Workflow 2: Adjusting filters and updating the results
1. User changes one or more controls in activity-filters-form (e.g., selects a different age group, month, time of day, or price limit).
2. User submits the filter form (e.g., clicks an "Apply filters" control).
3. activity-filters-form emits an onFiltersSubmit event with the new filter values.
4. url-state-manager updates the browser URL query string with the new values for ageMinYears, ageMaxYears, month, timeOfDay, and maxPricePerSession (and optional sort parameter, if implemented).
5. activity-results-list listens for filter state changes; when notified, it calls searchActivities with the updated parameters.
6. The list of activity-card components is re-rendered based on the new searchActivities response.
7. Since state is in the URL, the user can bookmark or share the current result set and restore it later.

Workflow 3: Viewing an activity detail and returning to the filtered list
1. User identifies an interesting activity in activity-results-list and clicks on its activity-card (or a dedicated "View details" control in the card).
2. activities-breadcrumb (or card-level navigation logic) constructs a returnQuery string from the current URL query parameters (including filters and optional sort) and URL-encodes it.
3. The browser navigates to activity_detail.html with parameters activityId set to the selected activity's activityId and returnQuery set to the encoded current query string.
4. On the detail page, a "Back to activities" link uses the provided returnQuery to navigate back to activities.html with the exact same query string.
5. When the user returns to activities.html, url-state-manager again parses the URL parameters, restoring the same filter state.
6. activity-filters-form reinitializes its control values from the restored filter state and activity-results-list re-calls searchActivities with these parameters, re-displaying the same result set.

Workflow 4: Adding activities to a registration list from the list view
1. User clicks a "Register" or "Add to registration" control on an activity-card.
2. If this is the first time addActivityToRegistrationList is used in the current page session:
   a. registration-preflight (within the activity-card or a shared controller) checks whether childName and numChildren are already known in page state.
   b. If not known, it opens a minimal inline form or modal asking for childName and numChildren.
   c. After the user submits this form, the values are stored in transient page state (not in localStorage) for reuse during this session.
3. The page calls addActivityToRegistrationList with activityId (from the selected card), childName, and numChildren.
4. On successful response, registration-list-indicator receives the updated registrationList, the addedItem, and totalItems.
5. registration-list-indicator updates its display (e.g., showing the current count of selected activities) without reloading the page.
6. The user may repeat steps 1–5 on multiple activity cards; subsequent calls reuse the same childName and numChildren unless the user explicitly changes them via a control exposed by registration-list-indicator.

Workflow 5: Proceeding to the registration list page
1. After adding at least one activity, registration-list-indicator becomes visible or shows a nonzero totalItems value.
2. User clicks a "View registration list" control within registration-list-indicator.
3. The browser navigates to activity_registration.html without additional parameters from this page (the backend identifies the correct ActivityRegistrationList via session or equivalent context).
4. If the user later returns from activity_registration.html to activities.html using standard navigation or a link that preserves the URL query, the previous filters remain active, and the list is restored as described in Workflow 3.

Workflow 6: Using browser navigation (back/forward)
1. User changes filters multiple times; each filter submission updates the URL query string.
2. User presses the browser Back or Forward buttons.
3. url-state-manager listens for URL changes (e.g., popstate events) and re-parses the URL parameters into filter state.
4. activity-filters-form updates its controls to reflect the filter values from the URL.
5. activity-results-list re-calls searchActivities with the current URL-derived filter state and updates the displayed activities accordingly.
6. registration-list-indicator retains its session-level state from prior addActivityToRegistrationList calls while the page remains loaded.
interactions: Changing any filter field in activity-filters-form (age group, month, time of day, maximum price) updates the local draft filter state but does not immediately call searchActivities until the user submits or an explicit apply action is triggered.

Submitting activity-filters-form triggers url-state-manager.onFiltersSubmit, which merges the submitted filter values into the URL query parameters and notifies activity-results-list to call searchActivities.
Clicking on an activity-card (outside of action buttons) triggers navigation to activity_detail.html with activityId and returnQuery based on the current URL query string.

Clicking a 'Register'/'Add to registration' control on an activity-card triggers the registration-preflight check for childName and numChildren, then calls addActivityToRegistrationList for the chosen activity.
Successful addActivityToRegistrationList responses cause registration-list-indicator to update its displayed totalItems and status, providing immediate feedback that the activity was added.
Clicking 'View registration list' in registration-list-indicator navigates to activity_registration.html to review and finalize the registration for all selected activities.

If the user modifies filters or sorting and then opens an activity detail, the activities-breadcrumb logic ensures the current query string (including updated filters) is embedded into returnQuery so the user can return to the same list state.

Using the browser Back/Forward buttons changes the URL; url-state-manager reacts by updating filter state and causing activity-results-list to re-run searchActivities, ensuring navigation history matches visible results.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The page uses URL query parameters as the single source of truth for filter and list-state persistence across navigation.

1. Reading state from URL on load:
- On initial load, url-state-manager reads the following URL parameters (if present): ageMinYears, ageMaxYears, month, timeOfDay, maxPricePerSession, and optionally sort (if sorting is implemented on this page).
- Each parameter is parsed and validated (e.g., numeric types for ageMinYears, ageMaxYears, maxPricePerSession; basic format checks for month and timeOfDay). Invalid values are ignored for filtering and not passed to searchActivities.
- The parsed parameters are stored in in-memory filter state and passed to activity-filters-form for control initialization and to activity-results-list for searching.

2. Deriving API call parameters:
- activity-results-list constructs the searchActivities argument object from the current filter state:
  - ageMinYears: from URL/state, or left undefined if not provided.
  - ageMaxYears: from URL/state, or left undefined if not provided.
  - month: from URL/state, or left undefined if not provided.
  - startDate: not used on this page and passed as undefined/null.
  - endDate: not used on this page and passed as undefined/null.
  - timeOfDay: from URL/state, or undefined if not provided.
  - maxPricePerSession: from URL/state, or undefined if not provided.
- searchActivities is invoked whenever filter state changes due to URL updates or initial load.

3. Updating URL from user actions:
- When the user submits the filters via activity-filters-form, url-state-manager merges new values into the URL query string:
  - ageMinYears and ageMaxYears are written only if selected; otherwise they are removed from the URL.
  - month is written if a month value is selected; otherwise removed.
  - timeOfDay is written if a time-of-day option is selected; otherwise removed.
  - maxPricePerSession is written if a price limit is set; otherwise removed.
  - sort (if present in the UI) is written whenever the user changes the sorting control.
- URL updates can be done using history APIs so the page does not reload. url-state-manager then re-derives filter state from the new URL to keep all components consistent.

4. Return navigation logic (returnQuery):
- Whenever an activity-card triggers navigation to activity_detail.html, the current query string (including ageMinYears, ageMaxYears, month, timeOfDay, maxPricePerSession, and optional sort) is URL-encoded and assigned to the returnQuery parameter.
- The detail page can later send the user back to activities.html with this returnQuery. On return, activities.html decodes the query string and applies it directly to the browser URL (or uses it as-is), which immediately restores the filter state and searchActivities behavior.

5. Registration state:
- addActivityToRegistrationList does not require or accept URL parameters and relies on backend/session context to associate items with the correct ActivityRegistrationList.
- The page keeps childName and numChildren in in-memory state only for the duration of the current page load; they are not serialized in the URL and not stored in localStorage.
- registration-list-indicator is updated purely from the latest addActivityToRegistrationList response (registrationList, addedItem, totalItems). On reload or fresh entry to the page, no initial registration list is fetched because no such interface is assigned; the indicator only reflects actions taken during the current session on this page.

components.functionality: Central controller that reads filter-related URL parameters on load, exposes them as filter state to other components, updates the URL when filters change, and reacts to browser navigation events to keep the filter state and displayed activities in sync.

Presents dynamic filter controls for age, month, time of day, and maximum price; initializes options from getActivityFilterOptions; pre-populates selections from url-state-manager; and emits a filtersSubmit event when the user applies filters so url-state-manager and activity-results-list can update.

Executes searchActivities based on the current filter state from url-state-manager and renders a collection of activity-card components, grouped or labeled by centerId/centerName. Re-runs searches when filters or URL parameters change.

Represents a single activity search result, displaying key information (name, date, time, center, age range, price) from searchActivities. Provides two main actions: open the detail page (with returnQuery) and add the activity to the registration list via addActivityToRegistrationList.

Collects or confirms the childName and numChildren values required for addActivityToRegistrationList when the user first attempts to add an activity. Stores these values in transient page state so subsequent add operations can reuse them without re-prompting unless the user chooses to edit them.

Displays summary information about the current activity registration list based on the latest addActivityToRegistrationList response, such as the total number of activities selected. Provides a navigation control to activity_registration.html when at least one activity is present.

Constructs navigation links related to the activities list. For each activity-card, it generates a returnQuery string from the current URL query to be passed along to activity_detail.html, so that the detail page can send the user back to the same filtered list.

Optional control group that allows users to select a sorting preference (e.g., by date, price, or center name). Updates a sort value in url-state-manager, which is reflected in the URL and used by activity-results-list to order the client-side display of searchActivities results.

layout.grid_system: Appears to follow a centered, fixed-width container on a fluid background, likely based on a 12‑column grid. The welcome section uses a two‑column grid (approx. 7 columns text, 5 columns illustration).
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows a linear, top-to-bottom flow with a clear hero-like control area at the top and a two-column content section below. At the very top of the content column, the breadcrumb sits left-aligned. Directly beneath it, a rounded, full-width filter "hero" houses the activity-filters-form as the main focal point. Below that, the page splits into a two-column layout similar to the mockup’s 'Üdvözlet' section: the left column holds the activity-results-list, and the right column acts as a lighter, supporting sidebar for sorting-controls and registration-preflight. A small floating registration-list-indicator anchors the bottom-right of the viewport, echoing the scroll-to-top pattern from the mockup.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows a linear, top-to-bottom flow with a clear hero-like control area at the top and a two-column content section below. At the very top of the content column, the breadcrumb sits left-aligned. Directly beneath it, a rounded, full-width filter "hero" houses the activity-filters-form as the main focal point. Below that, the page splits into a two-column layout similar to the mockup’s 'Üdvözlet' section: the left column holds the activity-results-list, and the right column acts as a lighter, supporting sidebar for sorting-controls and registration-preflight. A small floating registration-list-indicator anchors the bottom-right of the viewport, echoing the scroll-to-top pattern from the mockup.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The main content is contained in a centered, fixed-width container (approximately 1080–1200px) on a fluid, softly colored background. The top filter hero occupies the full content width with generous vertical padding (24–32px). In the two-column section, about 60% of the width is for the left results column and 40% for the right sidebar, reflecting the original 7/5 column split. Vertically, key sections (filters hero, results section, sidebar block) are separated by 48–64px, with inner elements spaced 16–32px apart, aligned with the given spacing scale.
layout_strategies.content_density: Controls and text are spacious and large, favoring easy scanning for busy parents. The filters hero uses big input fields and buttons with 8–12px internal padding and ample breathing room around them. Activity cards have 24–32px internal padding and a minimum 16–24px gap between cards, preventing the list from feeling crowded. The sidebar elements are grouped with clear separation so they read as distinct blocks, keeping the density low and approachable.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye starts at the breadcrumb and page heading area on the top-left, then moves rightward across the wide filter hero section, mirroring the hero headline+arrows relationship. From there, the gaze drops down into the left column where the activity-results-list and its individual activity-card items form the primary reading path. The right sidebar components (sorting-controls on top, registration-preflight below) serve as secondary focal points that users check intermittently. Finally, the floating registration-list-indicator at the bottom-right softly anchors the layout and reinforces the Z-pattern by providing an ever-present, tertiary visual hook.

layout.overall_description: The Activities & Holiday Programs page sits within a soft, pastel background with a centered content container and rounded, card-like sections echoing the warm, child-friendly mockup. At the very top of the content, a slim breadcrumb bar runs horizontally, left-aligned, giving way to a rounded rectangular filter hero that spans almost the full width of the container. This hero area is airy, with large, friendly filter fields and a prominent search action, sitting in a soft block with generous padding. Below, the page divides into a two-column layout: on the left, a comfortably spaced vertical list of rounded activity cards forms the main narrative flow; on the right, a slightly narrower sidebar stacks the sorting controls above a registration preflight summary, both styled as light, rounded panels with ample whitespace. Throughout, whitespace between sections and cards is generous, and vertical gaps are sizable, giving the screen a relaxed editorial feel. A small, rounded registration indicator floats near the bottom-right of the viewport, echoing the scroll-to-top button position from the original design and gently reminding users of their current selections without intruding on the main reading path.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The URL state manager is not visually represented in the UI; it operates behind the scenes within the page’s structure. Conceptually, it sits at the page level, governing the state of filters, sorting, and selections. It does not occupy any visible space, does not affect spacing between visual components, and is aligned with the overall layout only in terms of behavior: as users interact with the filter hero, sorting sidebar, results list, and registration components, the URL state manager synchronizes their states with the URL without adding any visual elements.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Invisible, page-level behavior that spans the entire main content area logically but does not render any visual element or consume layout space.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The activity-filters-form is placed near the top of the page as the main hero-like element. It sits directly below the breadcrumb, inside a rounded rectangular container that spans approximately 90–100% of the fixed content width, centered on the page. The container has generous internal padding (24–32px on all sides) and soft edges, visually echoing the hero slider from the mockup. The form’s fields are arranged in one or two horizontal rows depending on screen width: for example, date range and age range filters in the top row, with a keyword search and a large primary 'Search' button below, all aligned to the left but not cramped. Above the inputs (or aligned to their left), a large section title like 'Activities & Holiday Programs' can appear, forming the strong top-left focal point. There is a clear 24–32px vertical gap below this filters hero before the two-column results section begins.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the main content flow; it occupies the full-width hero section at the top of the main content. On scroll, it moves out of view with the page (not sticky), but retains a strong visual block with rounded corners and a soft background to distinguish it from the rest of the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The activities-breadcrumb sits at the very top of the main content column, above the filters hero, aligned to the left within the fixed-width container. It occupies a slim horizontal strip that spans roughly 80–100% of the content width but uses minimal height—just enough for one line of text and a compact icon or separator style. The breadcrumb has comfortable side padding consistent with the global container but no heavy box styling, ensuring it feels light and secondary. There is a modest vertical space of about 16–24px below the breadcrumb before the filters hero begins, preserving the clean, airy feel from the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, inline at the top of the content flow. It scrolls away naturally as the user moves down the page and does not remain sticky or floating.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The activity-results-list forms the main left column of the two-column section directly below the filters hero. This left column begins about 48px below the bottom of the filters hero to clearly separate the sections. The list’s column occupies approximately 55–60% of the total content width, aligned to the left of the container, reflecting the wider text column in the original 'Üdvözlet' layout. Inside this column, activity-card components are stacked vertically with a consistent vertical gap of 16–24px between each card, creating a comfortable rhythm. The top of the list can include a subtle count or description line (e.g., '12 activities found'), placed just above the first card with about 8–16px spacing. The right edge of this column aligns visually with the dividing line between the main and sidebar columns, leaving enough whitespace so that the list doesn’t feel pressed against the sidebar. As users scroll, this left column continues downward with evenly spaced cards, forming the core reading path.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static left column within a two-column layout. It scrolls normally while the sidebar may appear shorter or similar in height. On narrower screens, this column can collapse to full width above the sidebar content, but within the desktop layout it remains the dominant left segment.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each activity-card is a rounded, soft-edged block that fills nearly the full width of the activity-results-list column, leaving a small horizontal margin (e.g., 16px) on either side to keep the edges from touching the column boundary. The card has generous internal padding (24–32px on all sides) and a subtle, warm background tone, echoing the hero and welcome section cards from the mockup. Content inside the card follows a top-to-bottom structure: a large, friendly title at the top-left; beneath it, a short, bold summary line; then key details such as dates, times, age group, and location arranged in one or two rows with clear spacing (8–16px) between clusters. A call-to-action area (e.g., 'Add to list' button) is placed toward the bottom-right of the card, balancing the visual weight of the title on the top-left and reinforcing the Z-pattern within each card. Optional small icons or illustrative badges can sit on the right side of the card header area, maintaining the playful, child-focused visual language without overcrowding. Cards are separated from each other by 16–24px of vertical whitespace to preserve the low-density, editorial feel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block elements within the results list. They expand vertically as needed based on content but maintain a consistent maximum width dictated by the results column and do not have sticky or floating behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The sorting-controls occupy the upper portion of the right-hand sidebar in the two-column layout, mirroring the role of the illustration in the original mockup as a visually lighter but important counterpart to the main text column. This sidebar starts at approximately the same vertical position as the top of the activity-results-list, with about 60–70px of vertical gap below the filters hero to align the two columns comfortably. The sidebar’s total width is about 40–45% of the content container, aligned to the right, leaving a clear vertical gutter between it and the results column (roughly 24–32px). Within this right column, the sorting-controls are housed in their own rounded panel or stacked mini-panels with 16–24px padding, including elements like a 'Sort by' dropdown, view toggles, and maybe quick filter chips. These controls are arranged vertically, with each control given 8–16px of vertical spacing, maintaining the open, approachable feel. The top edge of the sorting-controls panel visually lines up with the first activity card’s top edge to reinforce column alignment.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Primarily static within the right sidebar. Optionally, on taller viewports, the sorting-controls panel can become sticky within its column once the user scrolls past the top, remaining visible while the left column scrolls; if implemented, it would stick with a small offset (e.g., 16–24px from the top of the viewport) but always within the sidebar boundaries.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The registration-preflight component sits directly beneath the sorting-controls in the right-hand sidebar, occupying the same column width (about 40–45% of the main container). It is separated from the sorting-controls by a vertical gap of about 24–32px, making it clearly a distinct block. The component is styled as a rounded card with a friendly, slightly more prominent background color than the sorting panel to signal its importance, but it remains visually lighter than the main content cards. Inside, a short, bold heading (e.g., 'Your Registration') appears at the top-left, with an overview of selected activities, total sessions, or key notices listed below in 8–16px spaced lines or bullet-like rows. A primary or secondary button (e.g., 'Review & Submit') is placed at the bottom-right within the card, anchoring the content and aligning with the action areas in the activity cards, supporting the page-wide Z-flow. The total height of this component is moderate; it should not rival the height of the results list but instead feel like a concise, reassuring summary.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the right sidebar, scrolling with the page. If the sorting-controls above become sticky, the registration-preflight scrolls behind or beneath them within the sidebar, remaining accessible once the user scrolls far enough down.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The registration-list-indicator is a small, floating status pill or rounded square that sits at the bottom-right of the viewport, echoing the scroll-to-top button placement from the original design. It overlays the content rather than occupying space in the normal flow. Visually, it is about the size of a compact chip or small square button, with soft, rounded corners and a warm accent color that stands out without being loud. It stays offset slightly from the edges (e.g., 24px from the right and 24–32px from the bottom), and contains a concise summary such as an icon plus a count of selected activities. On hover or tap, it can expand slightly in width to show a short label (e.g., '3 in list'), but even when expanded, it remains a small anchor element in the corner of the page. Its design is subtle enough not to compete with the main content but visually persistent enough to create a reassuring, ever-present anchor.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Floating and fixed relative to the viewport, independent of page scroll. It remains in the bottom-right corner as the user scrolls through results and the sidebar content, maintaining its offset from the viewport edges. It does not push or shift any other content, acting as an overlay element.

name: Activity Detail
title: Activity Detail
description: Displays full information for a single activity and allows the user to add it to their activity registration list while preserving navigation back to the filtered activities list.

core_features: Load and display detailed information for a single activity using getActivityDetails, including its schedule, age range, price, and hosting center.
Show basic information about the activity’s center (name, district, address, description) alongside the activity details.
Indicate whether the current activity is already in the user’s activity registration list using the isInRegistrationList flag from getActivityDetails.
Provide a registration form that captures childName and numChildren and, on submission, calls addActivityToRegistrationList for the current activityId.
Display confirmation and feedback after addActivityToRegistrationList runs, including the updated total number of activities in the registration list.
Offer a direct navigation link to the activity_registration.html page after a successful registration addition.
Provide a breadcrumb or back-link to activities.html that preserves the original filters via the returnQuery URL parameter when returning to the list.
user_workflows: View activity details: 1) User arrives on activity_detail.html with activityId (and optionally returnQuery) in the URL. 2) The page reads activityId from the URL and calls getActivityDetails. 3) While loading, a loading state is shown. 4) When data is returned, the page displays activity name, schedule (date and start/end times), age range, price, and center information. 5) If isInRegistrationList is true, the page indicates that this activity is already in the registration list.

Register for an activity: 1) After details load, the user sees a registration form with fields for childName and numChildren. 2) User enters the requested values. 3) On form submission, client-side validation checks that childName is filled and numChildren is a positive number. 4) If validation passes, the form triggers addActivityToRegistrationList with activityId from the URL and the entered childName and numChildren. 5) While waiting for the response, the submit button is disabled and a submitting state is shown. 6) On success, the page updates local state with the returned registrationList, addedItem, and totalItems, sets isInRegistrationList to true, and shows a confirmation message plus a link to view the registration list. 7) On error, the page shows an error message and re-enables the form.

Open registration summary: 1) After a successful addActivityToRegistrationList call, a “View registration list” link is available in the confirmation area. 2) When the user clicks it, the page navigates to activity_registration.html, where the current in-progress registration list is handled by that page’s own logic.

Return to filtered activities list: 1) User clicks the breadcrumb or back-link labeled to return to the activities list. 2) If a returnQuery parameter exists in the current page URL, the page navigates to activities.html with that query string appended so that the activities list can restore its previous filters. 3) If returnQuery is not present, the page attempts to navigate using browser history (e.g., going back one step). 4) The user sees the activities listing page with the same filters as when they originally opened the activity detail (as handled by activities.html).
interactions: On page load or when the activityId URL parameter changes, the page automatically calls getActivityDetails with that activityId and updates the UI with the returned activity and center data.
The user can click a breadcrumb or back-link labeled to go back to the activities list; this uses the returnQuery parameter from the URL, if present, to construct the target URL for activities.html.
The user can type into the childName input field; each keystroke updates form state and clears any validation errors linked to that field.
The user can change the numChildren input; changes update form state and trigger validation that the value is a positive number.
On submitting the registration form, if validation fails, the form shows inline error messages and prevents calling addActivityToRegistrationList until the issues are resolved.

On submitting the registration form with valid data, the form triggers addActivityToRegistrationList with activityId, childName, and numChildren and shows a loading state while waiting for a response.

On successful addActivityToRegistrationList, the page shows a confirmation notification containing information derived from registrationList, addedItem, and totalItems, and updates the UI to show that the activity is now in the registration list.

On failures from addActivityToRegistrationList (for example, due to server-side validation issues), the page shows an error notification and keeps the form inputs so the user can correct and resubmit.
After a successful registration addition, the user can click a link or button in the confirmation area to navigate to activity_registration.html to review or submit their registration list.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page state is driven by URL parameters and the outputs of getActivityDetails and addActivityToRegistrationList. On initial load, the page reads activityId and optional returnQuery from the URL query string. If activityId is missing, the page shows an error state and does not attempt to call getActivityDetails. If activityId is present, the data-loader component calls getActivityDetails(activityId) and stores the returned activity, center, and isInRegistrationList in in-memory page state. This state is used to drive all read-only display components and to determine whether the registration form's submit action should be enabled or labeled as already added. The returnQuery parameter is stored as-is in page state and used only when constructing the navigation link back to activities.html; it is never altered by this page. When the user submits the registration form, the current URL's activityId, along with the in-form childName and numChildren values, are passed into addActivityToRegistrationList. The response (registrationList, addedItem, totalItems) is stored in page state, which updates the confirmation banner and local view of the registration list count. After a successful add, the page sets a local flag so that isInRegistrationList is treated as true, even if the page is not reloaded. If the user later changes the activityId in the URL (for example, via client-side routing), the existing detail and registration state is cleared or treated as stale, and a new call to getActivityDetails occurs for the new activityId. The page does not use localStorage or other persistent client storage; all state besides URL parameters is held in memory and reset when the page is reloaded.

components.functionality: Initializes and manages loading of activity and center data based on the activityId URL parameter. Calls getActivityDetails(activityId) when the page loads or when the activityId parameter changes, and exposes loading, error, and data states to other components.

Displays a breadcrumb or back-link that returns the user to the activities list while preserving filters. Uses the returnQuery URL parameter when present to build a navigation URL for activities.html, and falls back to browser history if returnQuery is not available.

Shows the core details of the activity returned from getActivityDetails, including name, date, startTime, endTime, timeOfDay, ageMinYears, ageMaxYears, and an indicator if isInRegistrationList is true.

Displays the activity.description text from getActivityDetails if provided. If no description is available, the component hides the description area or presents a generic 'no description' state without using static content values.

Presents structured schedule and pricing information for the activity, such as the activity.date, startTime, endTime, timeOfDay, pricePerSession, and currency, using the dynamic data retrieved by getActivityDetails.

Displays information about the associated activity center obtained through getActivityDetails, including center.name, address, district, isNurseryCenter, and description, allowing parents to understand where the activity takes place.

Collects registration information for the current activity, specifically childName and numChildren. Performs client-side validation and, when valid, calls addActivityToRegistrationList with activityId from the URL and the entered values. Disables or alters the submit behavior if the activity is already in the registration list based on isInRegistrationList.

Displays success or error messages related to addActivityToRegistrationList. On success, uses registrationList, addedItem, and totalItems from the response to show that the activity was added and how many activities are currently in the registration list. Provides a 'View registration list' action that navigates to activity_registration.html.

Coordinates shared page state such as loading indicators, error states, currently loaded activity and center objects, derived isInRegistrationList status, current form values for childName and numChildren, and the latest registrationList-related data returned from addActivityToRegistrationList. Ensures that changes in URL parameters or interface responses are propagated to all dependent components.

layout.grid_system: Appears to follow a centered, fixed-width container on a fluid background, likely based on a 12‑column grid. The welcome section uses a two‑column grid (approx. 7 columns text, 5 columns illustration).
layout.layout_pattern: The page uses a linear, top‑to‑bottom flow inside a centered, fixed‑width container on a soft, fluid background. At the top, a hero‑like activity summary card (mirroring the mockup’s slider area) presents the title and key actions. Below, a broad two‑column band reproduces the 'Üdvözlet' pattern: the left column (about 55–60% width) stacks description, schedule & price, and the registration form; the right column (about 40–45%) holds the center info panel as the main illustrative/secondary focal area. The breadcrumb sits above the hero as a slim horizontal row, while loader, feedback, and state manager are layered behaviors rather than structural sections.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page uses a linear, top‑to‑bottom flow inside a centered, fixed‑width container on a soft, fluid background. At the top, a hero‑like activity summary card (mirroring the mockup’s slider area) presents the title and key actions. Below, a broad two‑column band reproduces the 'Üdvözlet' pattern: the left column (about 55–60% width) stacks description, schedule & price, and the registration form; the right column (about 40–45%) holds the center info panel as the main illustrative/secondary focal area. The breadcrumb sits above the hero as a slim horizontal row, while loader, feedback, and state manager are layered behaviors rather than structural sections.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: All content is centered in a max‑width container (roughly 1040–1120px), leaving generous lateral margins on large screens. The hero activity details span the full container width in a rounded rectangle with 24–32px internal padding. Below, the two‑column section allocates ~60% of width to the left content column (text + form) and ~40% to the right info panel, with 32–40px gutter between them. Vertical spacing follows the base 8px scale: 48–64px between major sections (breadcrumb → hero → two‑column band), and 24–32px between stacked cards within the left column.
layout_strategies.content_density: The layout is moderately sparse: large headings, comfortable line heights, and distinct separation between blocks. Each card (hero, description, schedule, form, center info) has 24–32px padding and clear internal grouping. Form fields are grouped with 16–24px vertical gaps, and text sections have 16–24px spacing between heading, intro, and body. There’s ample whitespace around the right‑hand center panel and between rows in the schedule, echoing the mockup’s open, child‑friendly editorial feel.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow follows a Z‑pattern: the eye starts at the top‑left on the activity title inside the hero card, then moves across to the top‑right where the primary 'add/register' action and key facts sit. From there, it drops diagonally down to the left column’s description heading, then flows downward through the schedule and the registration form. Throughout, the colorful center info panel on the right acts like the mockup’s illustration—pulling the eye over to the right and balancing the composition. Any scroll‑to‑top control (page‑level pattern) quietly anchors the bottom‑right, outside the main content cards.

layout.overall_description: The Activity Detail page sits in a soft, watercolor‑tinted background with a centered content column. A slim breadcrumb row at the top quietly situates the user. Just below it, a large, warm hero card with rounded corners showcases the activity name in a chunky, child‑friendly display type on the left, while the right side highlights key facts and a prominent 'add to list' button—echoing the mockup’s hero/slider structure. Scrolling down, the layout opens into a two‑column section similar to the 'Üdvözlet' design: on the left, a vertical stack of spacious cards presents the activity description, the detailed schedule and price, and then the registration form. On the right, a single, taller center info panel—decorated with a house/children‑style illustration and contact details—mirrors the role of the large illustration in the mockup, giving the page a friendly focal point. The columns breathe with generous gutters and vertical spacing, maintaining a low‑density, highly readable feel. When data is loading, a soft overlay with a centered playful loader gently dims the cards; when an action completes, a rounded feedback banner appears above the form, staying within the same warm visual language. The overall impression is of a modern, structured page that still feels handcrafted and welcoming for parents of young children.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The data loader appears as a full‑page overlay on top of the entire activity detail content area, starting just below the global site header and covering the centered content container edge‑to‑edge. It uses a semi‑transparent, slightly tinted backdrop that softens the underlying cards without fully hiding their rounded shapes, preserving the warm, watercolor atmosphere. At the center of the viewport, a moderately large, playful loading indicator (e.g., animated illustrated toy or spinning pastel circle) is displayed inside an invisible square footprint roughly 15–20% of the viewport width, leaving ample empty space around it. All other components remain in their positions underneath but are visually and interactively suppressed while the loader is visible.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed overlay: positioned fixed relative to the viewport, covering the full visible content area with a semi‑transparent background; appears above all other components and scrolls with the viewport (content behind does not move independently while active).

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The breadcrumb navigation sits at the very top of the Activity Detail content, directly beneath the site’s global header and above the hero activity details card. It is left‑aligned within the main centered container, spanning approximately 80–90% of the container width while remaining visually lightweight. Vertically, it occupies a slim strip with about 8–12px of padding above and below the breadcrumb text, and 16–24px of whitespace separating it from the hero card below. The style is understated—small, high‑contrast text with subtle separators—ensuring it provides orientation without competing with the large hero heading that follows.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static: part of the normal document flow at the top of the page content, scrolling away with the rest of the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The main activity details component forms a hero‑style card directly under the breadcrumb, echoing the mockup’s top slider area. It sits centered in the page, spanning nearly the full width of the fixed content container (roughly 90–100% of it), with generously rounded corners and 24–32px of internal padding on all sides. The background is a warm, light pastel block that floats above the page background, creating a clear focal section. Inside this card, content is arranged in a loose two‑column layout: the left portion, about 60–65% of the card’s width, holds the large activity title at the top, followed by a bold introductory sentence and key badges (e.g., age group, category, capacity) stacked with 8–16px spacing. The right portion, about 35–40% width, begins aligned to the top edge and contains compact, high‑value info such as next session time, quick price summary, and the primary 'Add to my registration list' button. This button is placed prominently in the upper‑right quadrant of the card, visually analogous to the mockup’s slider navigation area, drawing the eye across from the title. Below these, secondary details (tags, short notes) can run full‑width or remain within the left column, with at least 16–24px of breathing room before the next section starts. The card’s overall height is comfortably large, typically filling about one‑third to half of the initial viewport height depending on content density, preserving the open, editorial feel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static: occupies a fixed position in the vertical content flow between the breadcrumb and the two‑column detail sections.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The activity description section begins the two‑column band that mirrors the mockup’s 'Üdvözlet' layout. It is placed directly below the hero card with a vertical gap of about 48px. Within the main container, the content is split into left and right columns; the description belongs to the left column, which is 55–60% of the container width and aligned flush with the hero’s left edge. The description sits in its own lightly elevated card or directly in a subtly framed area, with 24–32px of padding and a soft background shade slightly different from the hero to indicate a new section. At the top of this card, a prominent heading (e.g., 'Tevékenység leírása') occupies the upper 20–25% of the card’s height, followed by a bold introductory paragraph that acts as a summary. The main body text flows beneath in one or two paragraphs, each separated by around 16–20px of vertical space to keep density low. Overall, the description card occupies the upper portion of the left column, visually aligning horizontally with the top of the center info panel on the right, setting up the same left‑text / right‑illustration rhythm found in the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static: first stacked card in the left column of the two‑column section, expanding vertically as needed based on text length.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The schedule and price component continues the left column directly under the description section, separated by about 24–32px of vertical whitespace to reflect a clear but related sub‑section. It uses a similar rounded card or softly bordered block, matching the 24–32px internal padding for consistency. Within this, the top 20–25% is dedicated to a concise heading and possibly a one‑line summary (e.g., 'Időpontok és díjak'). Below, individual schedule entries (days, times, sessions) are arranged in a vertical list with generous 16–24px spacing between rows. To echo the mockup’s numbered yellow circles, each major schedule row begins with a small, circular badge in a warm yellow tone on the far left of the row, containing either a short label (e.g., 'H', 'K') or a simple icon. The detailed text (day, time range, room, instructor) sits immediately to the right of each badge, occupying the majority of the row’s width. The price information is grouped either at the bottom or to the right of each schedule row in a slightly bolder style, but still within the same row height, ensuring the card remains visually light. The overall card height typically covers the middle portion of the left column content, leaving space beneath for the registration form.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static: second stacked card in the left column, following the description, with its content flowing downward as schedules are added.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The center info panel occupies the entire right column of the two‑column band, acting as the visual counterpart to the text‑heavy left column, much like the illustration of children in the mockup. Horizontally, it takes about 40–45% of the container width and is aligned with the right edge of the main content container, separated from the left column by a 32–40px gutter. Vertically, its top edge aligns roughly with the top of the activity description section, so that when this part of the page first comes into view, the user sees left‑side text opposite this right‑side panel. The panel itself is a tall, rounded card with soft pastel background and 24–32px padding. The top zone (upper 30–40% of the panel) may include a friendly illustration (e.g., a stylized drawing of the daycare building or playful icons) combined with the center’s name in display type, echoing the house‑and‑children motif from the mockup. Beneath this, structured info such as address, contact details, opening hours, and a small static map snapshot or icon grid is arranged in tidy blocks with 12–16px vertical spacing between rows. The panel typically stretches vertically to span roughly the same height as the combined description and schedule cards to its left, visually anchoring the right side of the layout and maintaining balance.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Optionally sticky within its column: on taller pages, the panel can use a sticky behavior with a top offset matching the main container’s padding (e.g., 24–32px), so it remains in view as the user scrolls through the left column’s schedule and form, while never overlapping the header.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The registration form is the final major block in the left column, sitting below the schedule and price card with a vertical gap of about 32px, signaling its importance as the primary action zone. It spans the full width of the left column (55–60% of the container) and is wrapped in a rounded, gently elevated card with 24–32px padding and a slightly more vivid pastel background to distinguish it from informational sections. The top of the form card features a clear heading (e.g., 'Jelentkezés a tevékenységre') and a short, reassuring line of text, occupying roughly the top 20–25% of the card’s vertical space. Below, form fields (child name, chosen session, notes, etc.) are arranged in a simple one‑column layout, each stretching nearly the full card width with 16–24px vertical spacing between groups of fields. Validation and help texts sit directly under relevant inputs with 4–8px spacing, keeping the overall layout open. The primary submit button ('Hozzáadás a jelentkezési listához') is placed at the bottom‑right inside the card, with enough whitespace above (16–24px) so it doesn’t feel cramped, mirroring the visual prominence of the main CTA in the hero but now tied directly to the detailed choices. The form’s height can extend to fill much of the lower viewport as needed, but internal padding ensures it always feels airy and approachable.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static: last stacked card in the left column; as the user scrolls, it moves normally, with no fixed positioning. It may be vertically adjacent to the lower portion of the right‑hand center info panel.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The registration feedback component appears in close proximity to the registration form so that confirmations or errors are immediately associated with the user’s action. Visually, it takes the form of a compact, rounded notification bar that spans most of the left column’s width (around 80–100% of the registration form card’s width) and sits directly above the form card with a 8–16px gap, or alternatively, as a band at the top of the form card itself. The notification has 12–16px of vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding, with a background color that clearly communicates status (soft green for success, warm yellow/orange for warning, etc.) while retaining the site’s watercolor, non‑aggressive aesthetic. An icon on the left (e.g., checkmark or exclamation in a small circular badge) anchors the message, and the text flows to the right, wrapping onto one or two lines with comfortable line height.
