Inside, a single large rounded rectangle acts as the main contact card, visually echoing the hero slider from the mockup. The card is divided into two stacked zones: a compact top header band (contact title + brief intro + nursery context indicator) and, directly below, a two-column content area where the contact form occupies the left column and supportive/contextual information (including the nursery context indicator on some breakpoints) sits on the right. Below the main card, a small back-to-home link sits aligned to the left. The submission-result panel reuses the same card footprint, replacing the form when active. Non-visual manager/loader components sit behind, controlling which of these states is shown.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The main contact card spans roughly 80–90% of the page width but never exceeds a fixed max-width (around 1120–1200px) and is centered horizontally. Within the card’s content area, the two-column layout allocates about 55–60% width to the left column (form fields) and 40–45% to the right column (context indicator and helper content), matching the mockup’s text/illustration balance. Vertical spacing follows the provided scale: 48–64px between the top navigation and the card, 24–32px padding inside the card, and 16–24px spacing between heading, intro text, and form sections. The submission-result panel uses the same outer dimensions and padding, but its inner content is centered with more breathing room, reducing perceived density.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density remains low to moderate. Form fields are large, with generous vertical spacing (16–24px) between them and comfortable line-height for labels and helper text. The title and short intro in the header occupy about the top quarter of the card’s height, with the remaining three-quarters given over to the form and right-side contextual area. The right column is visually lighter (fewer elements, more whitespace), echoing the airy illustration side of the mockup. The submission-result panel further reduces density: one clear heading, a short confirmation message, and perhaps one or two action links, all surrounded by ample whitespace.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Visual flow follows a loose Z-pattern inspired by the mockup. The eye first lands on the large contact heading in the top-left of the card, then scans to the top-right where the nursery context indicator sits, similar to the slider arrows in the original. From there, the gaze drops diagonally to the left column where the main form fields start, moving downward through the vertically spaced inputs. The right column acts as a secondary focal area, with the context indicator and any helper copy balancing the form visually. After submission, the confirmation message appears in the same top-centered region of the card, immediately capturing attention, while the back-to-home link anchors the lower-left of the layout.

layout.overall_description: The Contact / General Inquiry page sits on a warm, softly tinted background with subtle, child-friendly decorative elements at the edges. In the center of the viewport, a broad, rounded rectangle card floats with a slight shadow, echoing the hero slider shape from the mockup. At the top of this card, a large, playful heading announces the contact page, accompanied by a short, friendly line of text; together they stretch across roughly two-thirds of the width on the left, while a pill-shaped nursery context indicator hugs the top-right corner, taking the role of the mockup’s slider controls. Below this header band, the card transitions into a two-column layout: a wide left column containing the vertical flow of form fields, and a narrower right column that stays visually light, featuring the nursery context summary and any guiding hints in a calm, open arrangement. Generous padding inside the card, plus ample white space between sections, preserves the relaxed, editorial feel of the original design. When a submission is completed, the interior of the card gently switches to a centered confirmation state, simplifying to a single focal message. Beneath the card, aligned with its left edge, a small, understated back-to-home link rests with plenty of breathing room, grounding the composition as the last element in the vertical flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page-state-manager is an invisible, structural layer that wraps the entire central content area, including both the contact-form and the submission-result-panel. It occupies no visible space in the layout by default. In loading or transitional states (e.g., when submitting the form or initially fetching data), it can temporarily introduce a semi-transparent, warm-tinted overlay restricted to the main rounded contact card. This overlay sits on top of the form content, centered within the fixed-width container, slightly dimming the interior while leaving the surrounding page background visible. A small, softly rounded loading indicator can appear in the center of the card, maintaining the low-density, child-friendly aesthetic. Once loading completes, the overlay is removed, returning full focus to either the form or the confirmation panel without altering the underlying spatial relationships.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual wrapper in normal state; during loading, introduces a card-bound overlay that is position: absolute relative to the main card, covering 100% of its width and height without changing the overall page flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact-subject-loader is also non-visual and sits logically just above the contact-form within the main content hierarchy. It does not render any explicit UI elements itself, so it occupies no independent space. While subjects are being fetched, the loader’s presence is expressed through the form: the subject dropdown field in the left column shows a subtle skeleton or disabled state where the options would appear, using the same width as the input field and matching the vertical spacing of other form elements. This keeps the visual rhythm and spacing of the form consistent with the mockup’s comfortable layout while acknowledging the loading state.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Invisible data-loading layer that only influences the state of specific form controls; it does not change the structure or dimensions of the surrounding layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The nursery-context-indicator appears as a small, rounded badge or compact card positioned inside the main contact card at the top-right, mirroring the placement of the hero slider’s navigation arrows in the mockup. Horizontally, it aligns with the right edge of the fixed-width content container and sits flush with or slightly below the baseline of the main page heading on the left. It occupies roughly 15–20% of the card’s width and a low vertical height, ensuring it feels like a lightweight, secondary element. There is 16–24px of padding between it and the card’s top and right inner edges, and at least 24px of horizontal breathing room between it and the heading block on the left. On larger screens, it floats above the two-column area, overlapping visually with the form’s top region but not intruding into the fields. On smaller screens where the layout stacks, the indicator moves to a full-width row directly below the main heading and intro copy, centered or left-aligned with the form, maintaining the vertical flow while preserving generous top and bottom margins (around 16–24px) to keep the layout airy.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Positioned within the main card, effectively behaving as a right-aligned element in the header band; it is responsive, shifting to a stacked, full-width row above the form on narrow viewports.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact-form is the primary content block within the main rounded card and defines its overall presence. It begins just below the card’s top padding, starting with a large, warm, display-style heading aligned to the left, spanning roughly 60–70% of the card’s width. A short, bold or semi-bold introductory paragraph sits directly beneath the heading, limited to the same width, with 8–16px of vertical spacing between them, echoing the 'Üdvözlet' section structure. Below this intro, the layout transitions into a two-column grid: the left column occupies about 55–60% of the card’s inner width and houses all form fields (subject dropdown, nursery selector if present, message textarea, contact details). The right column uses the remaining 40–45% width and stays visually lighter, reserved for concise helper text, simple iconography, and the nursery-context-indicator when appropriate, maintaining ample whitespace around its contents. Vertical spacing between form groups in the left column follows the 16–24px rhythm, ensuring each block feels distinct and readable. The form extends vertically, occupying most of the card’s height and roughly the lower two-thirds of the visible viewport on desktop. At the bottom of the left column, the submit button area spans the full left-column width, with generous top margin (24–32px) separating it from the last field. On smaller viewports, the two columns stack: first the heading and intro, then the nursery-context-indicator (if not staying top-right), then the full-width form fields, followed by any right-column helper content, each separated by 24–32px to preserve the airy, editorial feel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows as a block-level element centered in the page, constrained to a fixed max-width and full responsive width within that bound; internally, it uses a responsive two-column grid that collapses to a single column on narrow screens.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The submission-result-panel occupies the exact same main card footprint as the contact-form but is shown only after a successful submission, replacing the form content within the rounded rectangle. It appears anchored at the top of the card’s inner padding, with a large confirmation heading centered horizontally at about 50–60% of the card’s width, mirroring the visual weight of the original hero headline. Below the heading, a short, reassuring paragraph and any key details (e.g., reference info or next steps) are stacked vertically with 8–16px spacing, all centered and limited to a moderate line length to maintain low density. The panel leaves generous empty space above and below this message block, so the content feels like it sits in the upper-middle portion of the card—roughly the central 40–60% of its height—creating a calm, celebratory state. If there are action controls (such as a button to send another inquiry or navigate elsewhere), they appear as a small horizontal group below the text, centered, with at least 24px of top margin. The panel thus preserves the same outer shape, padding, and general proportions as the form state but simplifies the internal composition to a single, prominent focal area.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Mutually exclusive with the contact-form inside the main card: when active, it fully replaces the form content while maintaining the card’s position and dimensions in the page flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The back-to-home-link is a subtle, text-based navigation element placed directly below the main contact card, aligned with the card’s left edge. It sits at a comfortable distance—approximately 24–32px of vertical space—from the bottom of the card, following the section margin pattern from the mockup. The link itself is compact, with a small icon or arrow and a short label, relying on color and perhaps slight underlining rather than heavy framing to keep it visually light. It does not compete with the main content; instead, it acts as a gentle endpoint to the page’s vertical flow. On very tall screens, when the card does not fill the viewport height, the link still remains just beneath the card within the central container, avoiding any floating behavior so the page retains its straightforward, top-to-bottom scroll. On narrow mobile screens, the link remains left-aligned within the container (or slightly indented), with enough surrounding whitespace to prevent crowding against the screen edges.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, inline-block element positioned in the normal document flow immediately after the main card, anchored to the left within the centered content container and not fixed or floating.

name: About the Municipal Nursery Service
title: About the Municipal Nursery Service
description: Informational page that loads and displays mission, values, coverage, and contact channels for the municipal nursery and daycare system, and routes users to key action pages.

core_features: Load mission, values, coverage statistics, and contact channels using the getAboutServiceContent interface on page initialization.
Display the municipal nursery service mission and values as separate, linkable sections.
Show high-level coverage information, including the total number of centers and the list of districts served, sourced dynamically from the interface.
List available contact channels (e.g., phone, email, in-person) with their labels and descriptions, sourced dynamically from the interface.
Provide contextual navigation links to "Find a nursery" (nurseries.html), "Fees & subsidies" (fees.html), and "Contact the nursery service" (contact.html).
Support deep-linking to specific sections (mission, values, coverage, contact) via URL parameters so external links can open the page at the relevant section.
user_workflows:
Workflow 1: Parent reads about the service and then searches for a nursery
1. User opens about.html (from footer or an external link without additional parameters).
2. The page triggers getAboutServiceContent and shows a loading state.
3. Once the data is returned, the mission, values, coverage summary, and contact channels are rendered.
4. User scrolls or uses the in-page section navigation to read the mission and values.
5. In the coverage summary or quick-links area, the user clicks the "Find a nursery" link.
6. The browser navigates to nurseries.html with a URL parameter indicating the origin, e.g., nurseries.html?source=about_service.
7. The user continues their journey on the nursery search page.

Workflow 2: Parent linked directly to coverage information
1. User follows an external link pointing to about.html?section=coverage.
2. The page reads the section URL parameter before or during initialization and stores it in page state.
3. The page triggers getAboutServiceContent and shows a loading state.
4. After data is loaded, standard sections are rendered.
5. The page automatically focuses or scrolls to the coverage section based on the section parameter.
6. The user reviews the number of centers and the districts served as provided by numCenters and districtsServed.
7. The user clicks the "Find a nursery" link inside the coverage section.
8. The browser navigates to nurseries.html?source=about_service&focus_districts=all (or another parameter scheme chosen by the implementation, without hardcoded district values).

Workflow 3: Parent wants to know how to contact the nursery service
1. User opens about.html (with or without a section parameter).
2. The page calls getAboutServiceContent and displays the contact channels list once data arrives.
3. User scrolls to or navigates to the contact section using in-page navigation or an external deep link like about.html?section=contact.
4. The contact channels list is shown, using the contactChannels array from getAboutServiceContent to render each channel with its type, label, and description.
5. The user may choose to reach out directly via one of the listed channels (e.g., by clicking a phone or email link if the implementation turns labels into actionable links), or clicks a "Contact the nursery service" navigation link.
6. The browser navigates to contact.html?source=about_service.

Workflow 4: Parent reads values and then checks fees and subsidies
1. User opens about.html, optionally with section=values.
2. Page loads data via getAboutServiceContent and renders all sections.
3. If section=values is present, the values section is highlighted or scrolled into view.
4. After reading, the user clicks a "Learn about fees & subsidies" link.
5. The browser navigates to fees.html?source=about_service.
interactions:
Page load interaction:
- Event: onPageLoad
- Action: Invoke getAboutServiceContent with no parameters.
- Result: Populate missionText, valuesText, numCenters, districtsServed, and contactChannels in the page state, then render all dependent components.

In-page section navigation interaction:
- Elements: Links or buttons such as "Mission", "Values", "Coverage", "Contact" in a simple in-page navigation.
- Event: onClickSectionNav(sectionId)
- Actions:
  1) Update the current URL’s query string to include section=<sectionId> (e.g., section=mission) without reloading the page (using history API), or with a full reload depending on implementation.
  2) Scroll or focus the corresponding section component.
- Result: Users can quickly jump between sections and share URLs pointing to a specific section.

Quick link to Find a nursery:
- Element: "Find a nursery" link rendered within the About content.
- Event: onClick
- Action: Navigate to nurseries.html with an origin parameter, e.g., nurseries.html?source=about_service.
- Result: User is taken to the nursery search/listing page with information that they came from the About page.

Quick link to Fees & subsidies:
- Element: "Learn about fees & subsidies" link.
- Event: onClick
- Action: Navigate to fees.html with an origin parameter, e.g., fees.html?source=about_service.
- Result: User is taken to the fees & subsidies page.

Quick link to Contact the nursery service:
- Element: "Contact the nursery service" link.
- Event: onClick
- Action: Navigate to contact.html?source=about_service.
- Result: User is taken to the general contact form or contact information page.

Contact channel interaction:
- Elements: A list of contact channel items generated from contactChannels (e.g., one item per channel).
- Event: onClickContactChannel(channel)
- Actions (implementation-specific and driven by channel.type and label):
  - Optionally construct actionable links such as tel: or mailto: based on channel.type and label.
  - Optionally update URL parameter channel=<channel.type> to reflect the user’s interest, e.g., about.html?section=contact&channel=phone.
- Result: Users see how they can reach the service and may trigger channel-specific actions.

Error and retry interaction:
- Condition: getAboutServiceContent fails to load (network or server error).
- Element: Retry button in an error state.
- Event: onClickRetry
- Action: Re-invoke getAboutServiceContent.
- Result: User can attempt to reload the About content without manually refreshing the entire page.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The About page maintains its state exclusively using the URL (no localStorage). The primary URL parameter is `section`, which controls which part of the page is emphasized.

- URL: about.html
  - Behavior: Default view. On load, the page calls getAboutServiceContent, and all sections (mission, values, coverage, contact channels) are displayed in their default order. No section is specifically highlighted beyond the default.

- URL: about.html?section=mission
  - Behavior: After getAboutServiceContent completes and missionText is available, the page automatically scrolls/focuses to the mission section and may visually indicate that it is the active section.

- URL: about.html?section=values
  - Behavior: After data is loaded, the page scrolls/focuses to the values section.

- URL: about.html?section=coverage
  - Behavior: After data is loaded, the page scrolls/focuses to the coverage summary that uses numCenters and districtsServed.

- URL: about.html?section=contact
  - Behavior: After data is loaded, the page scrolls/focuses to the contact channels list. Additional optional parameter `channel` may further refine the focus (e.g., highlighting a specific contact channel by type): about.html?section=contact&channel=<channelType>. The component responsible for the contact channels can then mark the matching channel (if any) as active.

- URL: about.html?section=<value>&source=<origin>
  - Behavior: The `source` parameter is informational and can be used by analytics or by other pages linking into about.html (e.g., from nurseries.html or fees.html). It does not change what content is shown, but the About page should preserve `source` when generating certain outbound navigation links if needed.

Outgoing navigation incorporates origin state using URL parameters:
- To nurseries.html: Links from this page should include a `source` parameter with a value that indicates they came from the About page (e.g., `source=about_service`). The About page itself does not interpret this parameter, but it allows nurseries.html to adapt its behavior if configured to do so.
- To fees.html: Similarly, links include `source=about_service`.
- To contact.html: Links include `source=about_service`. The contact page may use this to pre-fill contextual hints but that behavior is defined on contact.html, not here.

components.functionality: Central component responsible for invoking getAboutServiceContent on page initialization, managing loading and error states, and distributing the returned data (missionText, valuesText, numCenters, districtsServed, contactChannels) to child components via props or shared state.
event_handlers: onInit: Triggered when about.html is loaded; calls getAboutServiceContent.
onLoadSuccess: Receives the response object and updates the page state used by section components.
onLoadError: Sets an error state that can be consumed by a lightweight error display and retry control.
onRetry: Invoked when the user clicks a retry button; calls getAboutServiceContent again.

components.functionality: Provides in-page navigation controls (e.g., simple list of links) to jump to Mission, Values, Coverage, and Contact sections and keeps the URL's section parameter in sync with user selection.
event_handlers: onClickMission: Updates URL to about.html?section=mission (preserving other existing query parameters where appropriate) and scrolls to mission-section.
onClickValues: Updates URL to about.html?section=values and scrolls to values-section.
onClickCoverage: Updates URL to about.html?section=coverage and scrolls to coverage-summary.
onClickContact: Updates URL to about.html?section=contact and scrolls to contact-channels-section.
onURLChange: When the browser's URL changes (e.g., via back/forward navigation), the component updates the selected state and triggers the corresponding scroll/focus behavior.

components.functionality: Displays the mission of the municipal nursery and daycare service using missionText from getAboutServiceContent. Acts as a target for in-page navigation using the section URL parameter.
event_handlers: onDataUpdate: When missionText becomes available or changes, render or update the mission content.
onSectionFocus: When URLQuery.section == 'mission', ensure that this section is scrolled into view and optionally visually highlighted.

components.functionality: Displays the service values using valuesText from getAboutServiceContent and responds to section-based navigation.
event_handlers: onDataUpdate: When valuesText becomes available or changes, render or update the values content.
onSectionFocus: When URLQuery.section == 'values', scroll into view and optionally highlight this section.

components.functionality: Shows an overview of the service coverage using numCenters and districtsServed from getAboutServiceContent. Includes a contextual link to the nursery search page.
event_handlers: onDataUpdate: When numCenters or districtsServed change, re-render the coverage information without hardcoding any numbers or district names.
onSectionFocus: When URLQuery.section == 'coverage', scroll into view and optionally highlight this section.
onClickFindNursery: Navigate to nurseries.html?source=about_service (and optionally include additional parameters if required by the broader application, without hardcoding business values here).

components.functionality: Displays a list of available contact channels returned in contactChannels from getAboutServiceContent. Each channel shows its type, label, and description, and may be actionable depending on type.
event_handlers: onDataUpdate: When contactChannels are loaded or updated, render each channel item using the dynamic values (type, label, description).
onSectionFocus: When URLQuery.section == 'contact', scroll into view and optionally highlight this section.

onClickChannelItem(channel): Optionally update URLQuery.channel to channel.type and, if appropriate, trigger a channel-specific action (e.g., open a tel: or mailto: link) based strictly on dynamic channel properties.
onURLChannelChange: When URLQuery.channel changes, highlight the corresponding channel item if it exists.

components.functionality: Presents key action links derived from the page context: "Find a nursery", "Learn about fees & subsidies", and "Contact the nursery service", each leading to its respective page with origin information in URL parameters.
event_handlers: onInit: Optionally read and preserve any incoming source parameter when constructing outgoing URLs so that origin chains are maintained, without interpreting the value on this page.

components.functionality: Displays loading, error, or ready states based on the status of the getAboutServiceContent request.
event_handlers: onLoadStart: When getAboutServiceContent is called, show a loading indicator instead of the main content sections.
onLoadSuccess: When data is successfully loaded, hide the loading indicator and show the content sections.
onLoadError: When the interface call fails, display an error message and a retry control (without hardcoding message text, allowing localization or configuration).
onClickRetry: Trigger about-service-data-loader.onRetry to invoke getAboutServiceContent again.

Following the mockup, the page flows top-to-bottom in clear sections: (1) a slim, section-level header/navigation bar for this About page; (2) a primary About block where the mission plays the role of the 'Üdvözlet' section with a two-column layout; (3) subsequent content sections (values, coverage, contact) stacked vertically; (4) a compact quick-links area near the bottom. Headings are large and bold, intros slightly heavier, and body text uses comfortable line spacing with clear breaks between paragraphs and list items. No section feels cramped: each has visible whitespace above and below its content. Bullet lists and value items use ample vertical breathing room (16–24px between items). Cards and badges are simple and chunky, allowing the playful style to come through without visual noise.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow follows a gentle Z-pattern per viewport. Users start at the section navigation near the top-left, glance to any right-aligned controls or highlights, then move down into the mission two‑column block (headline and intro left, decorative/illustrative content right). As they scroll, the eye travels down through sectional headings on the left, with content blocks and occasional right-side emphasis (e.g., coverage illustrations) balancing each screen. A floating scroll-to-top control at the bottom-right (global, not part of this component list) quietly anchors the layout. The status indicator, when active, momentarily recenters attention to the middle of the container.

layout.overall_description: The About the Municipal Nursery Service page lives within a warm, softly colored background, with the main content held inside a centered, rounded-rectangle container that feels like a friendly card on the page. At the top of this card, a slim, playful section navigation bar introduces the page and offers quick jumps to Mission, Values, Coverage, and Contact, giving a clear starting point. Just below, the Mission section acts as the hero content for this page, echoing the mockup’s welcome layout: a large, welcoming headline and introductory copy on the left, paired with an illustrated panel on the right, all framed by generous padding and rounded corners. Scrolling down, the Values, Coverage Summary, and Contact Channels sections stack vertically, each separated by comfortable white space and following a rhythm: bold section heading, short intro, then well-spaced content lists or two-column layouts. The quick links near the bottom align slightly to the right within the same container, like a compact navigation helper that feels attached to the main content rather than a separate sidebar. Throughout, the composition remains light and airy, with rounded shapes, gentle spacing, and clear hierarchy, so that families can easily scan from one section to the next without feeling overwhelmed. If data is still loading or an error occurs, a soft overlay or centered status card temporarily takes focus in the middle of this content card, then retreats once the information is ready.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The data loader is not visually prominent when idle; it conceptually wraps the entire main content area of the About page. Spatially, it sits at the highest level of the page’s content container, spanning 100% of the container width from just below any global site header/hero down to the bottom of the About content. When active, it coordinates with the status indicator component to reserve space for a centered loading state, ensuring that the overall page height and section positions remain stable (no large jumps or collapses). There is no explicit card or box for this loader; instead, it governs when the section navigation, content sections, and quick links are visible, keeping the overall layout intact while content is being fetched.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Logical wrapper around the entire About page content; does not render a visible element by itself. Controls whether children components render or are temporarily replaced/overlaid by the status indicator.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The section navigation sits at the top of the About page’s main content container, occupying the full width of that container and positioned immediately below any site-level hero or main page title. It appears as a horizontally oriented, pill-shaped navigation bar within a softly rounded rectangle background that stretches edge to edge inside the container, with 16–24px of padding all around. The navigation items (e.g., Mission, Values, Coverage, Contact) are arranged from left to right, starting near the left edge; active or hovered items are emphasized with a chunky, rounded badge and a slightly stronger background color. There is 24–32px of vertical spacing between this navigation bar and the Mission section below, creating a clear separation while keeping them visually connected as part of the same introductory block.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard flow by default, but may become sticky on scroll: it can gently stick to the top of the viewport once the user scrolls past it, maintaining a small amount of vertical padding, so that section navigation remains accessible while not obscuring too much content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The mission section is the first major content block and visually acts like the 'Üdvözlet' welcome section from the mockup. It sits directly below the section navigation with approximately 32px of vertical gap. The section is housed in a rounded rectangle card that spans the full width of the main content container and uses 24–32px of padding on all sides. Inside, a two-column layout mirrors the mockup: the left content column occupies about 55–60% of the width, the right column about 40–45%. In the left column, a large, friendly heading (e.g., 'Küldetésünk') is placed at the top-left, followed by a short, bold introductory paragraph. Beneath the intro, 2–3 key mission points are listed vertically; each point may echo the numbered style from the mockup, using small round badges (soft yellow circles with 1, 2, 3) in a narrow sub-column aligned to the far left, with the corresponding text in a wider column to the right. There is 16–24px of vertical spacing between each mission point, keeping the feel airy. The right column contains a large illustration or graphic (e.g., children, caretakers, a house) centered vertically within the section, with generous whitespace on its sides so it doesn’t feel crowded. Overall height roughly fills a good portion of the first viewport when combined with the section navigation above, creating a strong, welcoming focal area for the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Part of the regular vertical flow. The two-column layout collapses to a single column on small screens, stacking text above the illustration, while maintaining 24–32px vertical spacing between them.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The values section is placed directly below the mission section, separated by approximately 48–64px of vertical white space. It also sits inside a rounded rectangle card spanning the full container width with 24–32px padding. At the top-left of the card, a bold section heading ('Értékeink') is followed by a short explanatory paragraph, each separated by about 16px. Below this intro, the values are presented in a loosely structured grid of 2–3 columns depending on screen width, with each value in its own small card or row. Each value item includes a small, friendly icon or mini-illustration on the left and a heading with one or two lines of text to the right. Items are arranged left-to-right then top-to-bottom, but with enough horizontal and vertical spacing (16–24px gutters) that nothing feels cramped. The entire section maintains the mockup’s low-density feel: large type, plenty of white space, and rounded corners on each value card. There is a clear bottom margin of around 32px inside the card, so the final row of values doesn’t hug the edge.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard stacked block within the flow. On narrow screens, the multi-column grid gracefully collapses to a single column with increased vertical spacing between value items.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coverage summary section follows the values section, with about 48–64px of vertical space between their cards. This section returns to a two-column layout similar to the mission section, emphasizing structured information on the left and a visual overview on the right. It sits in a full-width rounded rectangle with 24–32px padding. The left column (about 55–60% width) contains the section heading (e.g., 'Ellátási terület') and 1–2 short paragraphs that summarize the municipalities/districts covered, followed by a concise list of key stats (e.g., number of nurseries, number of groups). These stats can be arranged as a vertical list with subtle badges or chunky numbers to echo the numbered style from the mockup. The right column (around 40–45% width) holds a simplified map-style illustration or a schematic graphic of coverage locations, centered within the column with ample top and bottom whitespace so it reads as a calm, informative visual rather than an overload of detail. This right-hand visual gives the section a balanced, editorial feel and continues the eye’s Z-pattern across the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard two-column block in the vertical flow; on small screens, the text column stacks above the map/visual with 24–32px vertical spacing between them.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact channels section is placed below the coverage summary, again with about 48–64px of vertical spacing separating the cards. It uses a single-column, centered layout within a rounded rectangle that spans the container width, with 24–32px padding. At the top, a bold heading ('Elérhetőségek') is followed by a short, reassuring paragraph explaining how families can get in touch. Beneath this intro, the different contact channels (phone, email, in-person office, online form) are presented as a vertical list of compact cards or rows. Each contact row has a left-aligned icon (e.g., phone handset, envelope) and right-aligned text containing the channel label and details; rows span about 70–80% of the card width and are centered or left-aligned within the section to maintain a neat column. There is 16–24px vertical spacing between rows, ensuring they are easily tappable and readable. If there are primary actions (e.g., 'Call now', 'Write email'), they appear as rounded buttons aligned to the right of the corresponding row or immediately below the row text, echoing the mockup’s generous button padding (8–12px).
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard stacked block in the flow; rows become full-width on smaller screens, maintaining clear vertical spacing and touch-friendly sizing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The quick links component sits near the bottom of the main content, below the contact channels section, separated by roughly 40–56px of vertical space—slightly less than between major sections so it feels like a supporting element. It appears as a smaller rounded rectangle card aligned within the same main container, but visually anchored towards the right half to echo the mockup’s gentle Z-pattern: the card’s left edge aligns roughly with the center of the container, and the card spans about 40–50% of the container width. Inside, a compact heading (e.g., 'Gyors elérés') appears at the top-left of the card, with 8–16px of padding around it; below, 3–5 quick links are listed vertically with 8–12px internal padding per item. Each link is presented as a simple text row with a small right-facing arrow or playful icon, maintaining the rounded, chunky aesthetic. Generous whitespace around this quick-links card separates it from the page bottom and gives the impression of a helpful side note rather than another heavy section.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block within the vertical flow but visually offset to the right within the container. On narrow screens, it re-centers to span nearly the full container width, preserving its internal padding and spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The error and loading indicator appears only when triggered by the data loader. Spatially, it overlays or replaces the central portion of the main content container while preserving the page background and overall structure. When active in overlay mode, it is a medium-sized rounded rectangle centered horizontally within the viewport and vertically within the visible part of the main content area, covering about 40–60% of the container width. It has 24–32px of internal padding, with a simple icon or spinner at the top center, followed by a short heading ('Betöltés folyamatban...' or an error message) and a brief explanatory line. The rest of the About page behind it is either dimmed slightly or remains visible but visually subdued, ensuring user focus is on the status card. When used inline (e.g., on hard errors), it occupies the top part of the content area where the Mission section would normally begin, with full-container width but still in a rounded rectangle, maintaining consistent spacing above and below. In both cases, it respects the mockup’s low-density, friendly style, avoiding cramped text or aggressive alerts.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Temporarily overlays or replaces the main content within the central container while active, centered both horizontally and approximately vertically. Non-sticky; it disappears once loading resolves or the error is dismissed/recovered, returning the layout to the regular stacked sections.
